description: Express/one-tap checkout (Feature 15), Savings summary in cart/checkout (Feature 18), Notification preferences granularity (Feature 19), and Multi-language integration into UI (Feature 11).
steps: Create apps/web/components/cart/express-checkout.tsx: 'Quick Order' button that appears when user has a default address within delivery radius and has placed at least one order before. Shows the default address, total, and payment method in a compact card. One tap triggers order placement by calling /api/orders with saved preferences. Uses Framer Motion slide-up animation.

Update apps/web/components/cart/cart-page-client.tsx: Add ExpressCheckout component at the top of the cart page for eligible users. Add a 'Savings Summary' section showing total savings: calculate sum of (price - discountPrice) * quantity for all discounted items in cart. Show as a green highlighted banner 'You are saving Rs X on this order!'.

Create apps/web/app/api/cart/express-eligible/route.ts: GET endpoint that checks if the current user is eligible for express checkout (has default address within radius, has completed at least 1 order). Returns { eligible: boolean, address: {...}, paymentMethod: string }.
Update apps/web/components/checkout/order-summary.tsx: Add a savings summary line showing total discount amount in green. Calculate from items where discountPrice < price.

Update apps/web/components/account/settings-client.tsx: Add new notification preference toggles: 'Price Drop Alerts' (priceDropAlerts), 'Weekly Deals' (weeklyDeals), 'Delivery Status Alerts' (deliveryAlerts). These map to the new fields added in schema. Update the PATCH call to include new fields.
Update apps/web/app/api/account/settings/route.ts: Handle the new notification preference fields (priceDropAlerts, weeklyDeals, deliveryAlerts) in the PATCH handler.
Update apps/web/app/layout.tsx: Integrate next-intl NextIntlClientProvider. Wrap the app with the provider. Read locale from cookie or default to 'en'. Import messages for the detected locale.
Create apps/web/middleware.ts update: Add locale detection logic (check 'locale' cookie, fallback to 'en'). Set locale in request headers for server components.

Update key customer-facing components to use useTranslations from next-intl: apps/web/components/header.tsx (store name, navigation labels), apps/web/components/home/home-search.tsx (placeholder text), apps/web/components/cart/cart-page-client.tsx (cart labels, totals), apps/web/components/checkout-form.tsx (form labels, buttons). Use t('key') pattern throughout.
Add the language switcher to the header (apps/web/components/header.tsx) or account settings page.
acceptance_criteria: Express checkout button appears for eligible users on cart page
Savings summary shows total discount in cart and checkout
New notification preferences (priceDropAlerts, weeklyDeals, deliveryAlerts) have toggles in settings
Settings API handles new notification fields
next-intl provider wraps the app with locale detection
Key customer pages show translated strings when locale is 'ml' (Malayalam)
Language switcher is accessible from header or settings
verification: cd apps/web && npx next build 2>&1 | tail -20
findings: Build passes cleanly. next-intl integrated via createNextIntlPlugin in next.config.ts and NextIntlClientProvider in layout.tsx. The non-routing (cookie-based) locale approach works as expected. All new notification preference fields were already in schema. useTranslations added to header, home-search, cart-page-client, and checkout-form.
